channel.title: Media Discussion (new threads)
channel.description: Threads in the forum category "Media Discussion" - TV, Movies, Books and other noninteractive media, including creepypasta!

item.title: A REALLY underrated podcast
item.content:encoded:
I found this podcast not that long ago but the videos in the podcast have really good discussions in them and really good editing. Yes the podcast/channel IS new but it still bothers me so go take a look at it if you don't like it leave if ya like it sub and show the support this man needs
Here's the link to the channel:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC3-tiMJw4PmyfQR-PlrGvZg

item.title: Favorite horror movies
item.description: Since we’re a community of discerning horror taste.
item.content:encoded:
What are your favorite horror movies?

My favorite is of course The Shining, though I also enjoy Halloween (1978). A Cure for Wellness had a lot of potential, but it fell apart in the second half.

item.title: So Scarlet King is literally Aku from Samurai Jack?
item.content:encoded:
Shapeshifting master of darkness, wants to unleash an unspeakable evil. Also, they both are horn-y. (But King is specifically horny.)
They both have daughters who are capable of destroying him, one betrays him.
So is 999 going to become a samurai wielding a magical sword of magic of love, hope and compassion?

item.title: Favorite Music?
item.content:encoded: [Post Body]

item.title: Music Discussion
item.content:encoded:
Can't believe this wasn't already here! I'm looking to start a discussion about the different music you like, or at least what you've been listening to recently.

Personally, I've been listening to stoner/doom metal. Sleep and Uncle Acid and the Deadbeats in particular, but Pandora's algorithm and 666MrDoom's YouTube channel haven't disappointed me yet.

item.title: The Scariest Animation(s) on the Internet
item.content:encoded:
This video is the scariest thing I have ever seen. Anyone else got similar videos like this one?

Click to view the horror

item.title: Is there any websites similar to the SCP Wiki?
item.description: I want to find websites that are similar to the SCP Wiki, with the Series stuff and the anomalous objects and stuff. If you could recommend any websites, then post them in this thread.
item.content:encoded:
I already made a site inspired off of the SCP Wiki, which can be found here. Besides that, are there any similar websites? Not the Wanderers' Library, as I am already a member of that site.

Oh and if you want to join my SCP inspired wiki, go right ahead. You don't have to.

item.title: Arai-san Mansion
item.description: Kemono Friends fan comic in Silent Hill-ish apartment
item.content:encoded: I discovered this on Pixiv yesterday, and just found English translated version today.

item.title: James Randi is dead
item.content:encoded:
The great debunker is now rest in peace.

I can't help but think that in Foundation-Verse, he was the one staff who contribute to more than many -EX classification (and many more that not even get skip status in the first place).

item.title: SCP Games
item.description: Do you guys know about any obscure but good SCP games?
item.content:encoded:
I first got into SCP because one of my friends wanted me to play Secret Lab with him (a game I know love and think is a little underrated). I’ve played pretty much all of the popular ones like CB, SCP Unity, SCP 087, SCP Terminal, and Fractured Minds. I think I might’ve played a few others. I was wondering if any of you guys know about any hidden gems (though I don’t expect anyone to), because I really like most of the SCP games that I’ve played before (even though they may not be a accurate portrayal of the modern SCP website, they’re still fun so please don’t bully) and was wondering if there’s anything left.

Anyways remember to eat your trees kids. It’s good for your leg Muscles.

item.title: Spriggan anime coming to Netflix
item.content:encoded:
ANN link

Basically, it would be what happen if the Foundation and GOC actually merge with some element of shonen manga.

item.title: I made an Entity on an SCP-like wikia site
item.content:encoded:
So, I write for the Backrooms wiki, and this entry called 'Entity 99' is one of my most iconic on the site. I was wondering what the SCP community thought of the entry, just out of curiosity!

http://backrooms-wiki.wikidot.com/entity-99

Apologies if this breaks any rules or is in the wrong spot!

item.title: Flashpoint Just league Dark
item.content:encoded:
So I’ve seen parts of Justice League Dark Apokolips (which was, a bit to dark for me.) but I know Barry travels back in time to try and change stuff but I’m not clear whether this actually resets things, or just creates an alternate timeline.
I would like it to reset because honestly, maybe I’m too soft or super edge brutal killing isn’t me, but I hope it’s a reset. I just couldn’t really like seeing characters I’ve loved for years die so indignantly. Starfire was the one that definitely made me cringe, Grayson and her built up something and she’s mechanized after being torn in two. And I like the previous movies, but this was too much for me. Call me queezy, I have a threshold for dark. Especially when it comes to beloved characters. MK they always die gruesomely so that never bothers me. So I want more movies but with the reset.

item.title: Looking for pationate sci-fi and horror writers.
item.content:encoded:
Hello,

I am representative of Creaky Corpse ltd, small indie company, mainly known for Dead Frontier games. We are looking for somebody keen on writing horror fiction genre, to write a story line for one of our zombie apocalypse games in form of notes, scientific reports, newspapers, diaries etc. In other words, we are looking for style similar to SCP articles.
DF universe already has some lore and timeline which we would like to be used as foundation for new story line but it is subject to change.

If you are interested please feel free to send me a PM or post here with examples of SCP articles you made and contact info (preferably discord). And yeah it is paid job :)

~ Anklast

item.title: SCP Foundation? What SCP Foundatin? This is the PCS Foundation!
item.description: A discussion on how to create a universe where anomalous objects and an organization captures them and research them.
item.content:encoded:
Hello Hello! This is just a general thought that I have in my mind.

We all love our wonderful SCP Foundation universe. We love the way the system work and the anomalous objects. So much so that we can potentially recognize those same things in this other works.

There is this piece of media (I won't say the name but you can guess) that came out in 2019. It's about a world where anomalous objects exist and there is an American organization that is dedicating to capturing these objects and researching them. In this universe, this organization takes place in a building that mysteriously appeared a long time ago and looks super creepy.

+ show block
– hide block

Now when this game came out, a lot of people made comparisons to the SCP Foundation. Rightly so, as it did borrow a few things from the SCP universe. For example, there are several objects that are just ordinary but have weird qualities to them (There is a fridge that you have to stare at or else it starts to kill people). There is also an overwhelming sense of bureaucracy with files upon files of information related to any anomalous object of note.

So here comes the question, is it possible to create a universe where anomalous objects exist there is an organization (or several organizations) that seek to capture them and research them while being distinct from the SCP Universe?

Feel free to discuss this in the thread but I would like to throw in my own two cents. I think the best thing you can do when it comes to making sure your universe is distinct from the SCP universe is clarity and specifics. So much of the SCP Universe is shrouded in mystery. While there are a lot of concrete specific definitions for there specific things (Clearance Levels, Mobile Task Forces, etc) there is still a lot of mystery. If you read the O5 Command Dossier, you don't see descriptions for one person who is specifically labeled O5-X, you see an entire list of people who could potentially be O5-X. Hell, you can see a note saying that the O5 council might not be real.

When I say that clarity and specifics can be used for distinction, what I mean is that you can have a lot of explanations and worldbuilding in your universe. How did these objects come to be? Why are they like that? Are there multiple reasons why or is there just one big reason why? How did the world react to them? Do people like these objects or not? All of these standard worldbuilding questions can be used to make your world feel more different from the SCP Universe. Specifics and clarity make your idea yours.

It also helps not to straight-up steal concepts from the website. I know that might seem kinda hard since there is so much content on the site (5000+ entries and so on), you might accidentally rip off someone but as someone who has an entire list of SCP ideas on a Google Doc and who also see's which ideas have been done before (Spoiler alert, a lot of them haven't been done yet), I think it is much easier to make an idea and add specifics to that idea in order to make it your idea than to just take something wholesale from the site. Especially if you are just stealing from the Hall of Fame section.

What do you guys think? Feel free to discuss this!


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

item.title: Super Mario Odyssey but 682
item.content:encoded: What if SCP-682 was in Super Mario Odyssey, and Mario threw the hat at him. Do you think SCP-682 could adapt somehow, or do you think he would be hopeless?

item.title: Tom Scott's Amazing Places series
item.description: Also, the wiki got a shoutout!
item.content:encoded:
So YouTuber Tom Scott gave qntm (author page here: http://www.scpwiki.com/qntm-s-author-page), and indirectly the SCP wiki, a shout-out in a video posted today: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hr03xF08qoU ("Is The Most Northern Part Of Iceland Still There?")

Tom Scott actually has a bunch of videos in his Amazing Places series that make pretty great SCP fuel—here are my personal favorites:

  • The Poison Garden of Alnwick: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jGo9gYypQc8
  • The Broken Building That Must Not Be Destroyed: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AtkmDmMzZO8 (there's actually some comments about the title sounding like that of an SCP object)
  • The Lava Lamps That Help Keep The Internet Secure: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1cUUfMeOijg (esoteric containment procedures, anyone?)
  • I Hit 3,000-Year-Old Art with a Hammer: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=s6kNR8Mj0ZE (aka The White Horse, in Uffington)

Does anyone else watch this series?

item.title: Looking for a quote
item.content:encoded: Something-something being analogoueus to being "blindfolded and strapped onto to the broken steering wheel of a burning boat in the middle of a storm." This isn't the quote btw, it's the image that gets invoked when being read.

item.title: Inforgraphic show SCP videos
item.content:encoded: Just wanted to mention, credit where its due, that they did a much better job with this overview one than the first.

item.title: Have you ever dreamt up an SCP?
item.content:encoded: I just woke up from a nightmare about an SCP so a lot of details were lost but there was a woman with her lesbian assistant who lured assholes into her building. Once inside she would summon insects to devour them. My nightmare protagonist was in the wrong place at the wrong time and managed to escape to the exit with the lesbian assistant. The protagonist didn't want to hurt the Insect woman because it was an accident that she entered the web. But because of how the protagonist stalled the insect woman began physically breaking down into a Spider human hybrid stuck on the ceiling as the room filled with webs and spiders so the protagonist dragged the assistant out and called the foundation who secured the building.( And before anyone says spiders aren't insects they're arachnids in the first room she summoned flesh eating snails so it wasn't just spiders)
